@@ -58,7 +58,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","title":"Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step","description":"# Task: Integrate preflight into install.sh as optional first step ## Context Part of EPIC: Pre-Flight Validation (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0ok) ## Rationale Users who skip the wizard and run the installer directly should still get pre-flight validation. Currently they only see failures 15 minutes in. ## Proposal Add preflight as Phase 0 of install.sh: ```bash run_phase 0 "Pre-Flight Validation" run_preflight_checks ``` ### Behavior - Run all preflight checks - On FAIL: Show errors, exit (don't waste time) - On WARN: Show warnings, ask to continue (unless --yes) - On PASS: Continue silently ### Flag ```bash --skip-preflight # Skip pre-flight checks (for debugging) ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - Preflight runs before Phase 1 - Failures abort immediately with actionable message - Warnings shown but don't block (with --yes) - --skip-preflight available for edge cases ## Files to Modify - install.sh: Add Phase 0, add flag - scripts/preflight.sh: Ensure can be sourced, not just executed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:00.154616-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:47:37.034619-05:00","close_reason":"Integrated preflight into install.sh as Phase 0. Added run_preflight_checks() function, --skip-preflight flag, and auto-download for curl|bash scenario.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-545","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0iq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.766175-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-58by","title":"Refactor install_helpers.sh to remove duplicate feature-flag logic","description":"scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh currently contains duplicated/overlapping implementations for generated-vs-legacy category feature flags (multiple helpers and a later redefinition of acfs_use_generated_category). Consolidate to a single implementation, remove dead code, and add a small shell test to lock behavior.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:08:53.010083-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:56:22.523356-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (generated category flag logic consolidated)"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","title":"Task: Create command reference page","description":"# Create Command Reference Page ## Parent Feature Reference Documentation (1y8) ## Goal Create a searchable command reference page at `/learn/commands` that lists all commands installed by ACFS. ## Design ### Page Layout ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Command Reference │ │ │ │ [🔍 Search commands...] │ │ │ │ Category: [All] [Agents] [Search] [Git] [System] │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ cc [Copy] │ │ │ │ Claude Code - AI coding assistant │ │ │ │ Example: cc "fix the bug in auth.ts" │ │ │ │ [Full docs →] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ cod [Copy] │ │ │ │ Codex CLI - OpenAI coding agent │ │ │ │ ... │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ ... │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Command Data Structure ```typescript // lib/commands.ts interface Command { name: string; fullName: string; description: string; category: 'agents' | 'search' | 'git' | 'system' | 'stack'; example: string; docsUrl?: string; aliases?: string[]; } export const COMMANDS: Command[] = [ // Agents { name: 'cc', fullName: 'Claude Code', description: 'AI coding assistant', category: 'agents', example: 'cc "fix the bug"' }, { name: 'cod', fullName: 'Codex CLI', description: 'OpenAI coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'cod "add tests"' }, { name: 'gmi', fullName: 'Gemini CLI', description: 'Google coding agent', category: 'agents', example: 'gmi "review code"' }, // Search { name: 'rg', fullName: 'ripgrep', description: 'Ultra-fast code search', category: 'search', example: 'rg "pattern" ./src' }, { name: 'fd', fullName: 'fd-find', description: 'Fast file finder', category: 'search', example: 'fd "*.ts"' }, { name: 'fzf', fullName: 'Fuzzy Finder', description: 'Interactive fuzzy search', category: 'search', example: 'fzf' }, // Git { name: 'lazygit', fullName: 'LazyGit', description: 'Visual git interface', category: 'git', example: 'lazygit' }, { name: 'git', fullName: 'Git', description: 'Version control', category: 'git', example: 'git status' }, // System { name: 'ntm', fullName: 'Named Tmux Manager', description: 'Session management', category: 'system', example: 'ntm new project' }, { name: 'lsd', fullName: 'LSDeluxe', description: 'Modern ls replacement', category: 'system', example: 'lsd -la' }, { name: 'bat', fullName: 'Bat', description: 'Cat with syntax highlighting', category: 'system', example: 'bat file.ts' }, { name: 'zoxide', fullName: 'Zoxide', description: 'Smart cd replacement', category: 'system', example: 'z project' }, // Stack { name: 'bd', fullName: 'Beads CLI', description: 'Task graph management', category: 'stack', example: 'bd list' }, { name: 'bv', fullName: 'Beads Viewer', description: 'Kanban task viewer', category: 'stack', example: 'bv' }, { name: 'ubs', fullName: 'Ultimate Bug Scanner', description: 'Code quality scanner', category: 'stack', example: 'ubs .' }, { name: 'cass', fullName: 'CASS', description: 'Session search', category: 'stack', example: 'cass' }, { name: 'cm', fullName: 'CASS Memory', description: 'Procedural memory', category: 'stack', example: 'cm context "auth"' }, { name: 'caam', fullName: 'CAAM', description: 'Account manager', category: 'stack', example: 'caam status' }, { name: 'am', fullName: 'Agent Mail', description: 'Agent coordination', category: 'stack', example: 'am status' }, ]; ``` ### Features 1. **Search** - Filter by command name or description 2. **Category tabs** - Quick filter by category 3. **Copy button** - One-click copy command 4. **Expandable** - Show full usage on click 5. **Deep links** - `/learn/commands#cc` jumps to command ### SEO - Title: "ACFS Command Reference" - Description: "Quick reference for all commands installed by Agent Flywheel" - Keywords: individual command names ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Page accessible at `/learn/commands` - [ ] All ACFS commands listed - [ ] Search filters results in real-time - [ ] Category tabs work - [ ] Copy button copies command - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/lib/commands.ts` - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/commands/page.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/command-ref-card.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:22:20.811137-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:46:18.156226-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented /learn/commands with search, category filters, deep links, and copyable examples.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5gq","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1y8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:10.574895-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","title":"Add SSH key verification step (check files exist)","description":"# Task: Add SSH Key Verification Step ## Parent Epic [EPIC] SSH Key Generation Clarity ## Problem After running ssh-keygen, users don't know how to verify it worked or understand what was created. They have two files (public/private) but don't know which is which or what they're for. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Add verification section after the command: \`\`\`tsx

You should see two files:

  • acfs_ed25519 — Your private key (keep this secret!)
  • acfs_ed25519.pub — Your public key (this gets shared)
 Think of it like a mailbox: the public key is your address (you share it), the private key is your mailbox key (only you have it). \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows verification command - [ ] Explains the two files and their purposes - [ ] Uses relatable analogy (mailbox) - [ ] Reassures user about what to keep secret ## Why This Matters Users who don't verify their key might not realize when something went wrong. The public/private distinction prevents confusion later when they need to share their public key.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-unqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:12.253977-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","title":"Add SSH key verification step (check files exist)","description":"# Task: Add SSH Key Verification Step ## Parent Epic [EPIC] SSH Key Generation Clarity ## Problem After running ssh-keygen, users don't know how to verify it worked or understand what was created. They have two files (public/private) but don't know which is which or what they're for. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx Add verification section after the command: \`\`\`tsx

You should see two files:

  • acfs_ed25519 — Your private key (keep this secret!)
  • acfs_ed25519.pub — Your public key (this gets shared)
 Think of it like a mailbox: the public key is your address (you share it), the private key is your mailbox key (only you have it). \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows verification command - [ ] Explains the two files and their purposes - [ ] Uses relatable analogy (mailbox) - [ ] Reassures user about what to keep secret ## Why This Matters Users who don't verify their key might not realize when something went wrong. The public/private distinction prevents confusion later when they need to share their public key.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:53:09.415672-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:25:36.905528-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5i2y","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-unqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:12.253977-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","title":"EPIC: VPS Installer (install.sh)","description":"# Epic: VPS Installer (install.sh) ## Overview Build the one-liner bash installer that transforms a fresh Ubuntu VPS into a fully-armed agentic coding environment. ## Background & Reasoning This is the "magic moment" - the user pastes one command and everything happens. The installer must: - Work when run as root (common VPS default) - Work when run as ubuntu with sudo - Be idempotent (safe to run twice) - Handle connection drops and resume - Log everything for debugging ## Design Philosophy **Vibe Mode Default**: Passwordless sudo, agents with full permissions, minimal friction. **Checkpointed Phases**: Each phase writes state so reruns skip completed work. **Module Manifest**: Single source of truth (acfs.manifest.yaml) drives all installs. ## Installer Phases 1. Preflight + confirm OS (Ubuntu 24.04+ / 25.x) 2. Create/normalize `ubuntu` user + sudoers 3. Base packages (apt) 4. Shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh + p10k + plugins) 5. Dev toolchain (bun, uv, rust, go, tmux, rg, ast-grep) 6. Agent stack (claude/codex/gemini CLIs + aliases) 7. Flywheel stack (ntm, slb, ubs, cass, cm, bv, caam, mcp_agent_mail) 8. Post-install UX (onboard + doctor + summary) ## Success Criteria - [ ] Fresh Ubuntu 25.x VPS → working environment in < 15 minutes - [ ] Safe to run twice without breaking anything - [ ] All 8 Dicklesworthstone tools installed and verified - [ ] `acfs doctor` shows all green - [ ] Logs available at /var/log/acfs/install.log ## Considerations - Always prefer upstream installers when available - Verify each tool after install - Provide clear error messages with fix suggestions - Support `--dry-run` for transparency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:01:44.001564-05:00","close_reason":"All 12 sub-tasks completed. VPS installer fully modularized with libraries for: logging, OS detection, user normalization, shell setup, CLI tools, language runtimes, coding agents, Dicklesworthstone stack, cloud/database tools, smoke test, and canonical zshrc.","labels":["epic","installer"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","title":"Create installer logging library (scripts/lib/logging.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Installer Logging Library ## Description Build a reusable logging library for consistent installer output. ## Functions ```bash log_step() # [1/8] Step description (blue) log_detail() # Indented detail (gray) log_success() # ✔ Success message (green) log_warn() # ⚠️ Warning message (yellow) log_error() # ✖ Error message (red) log_debug() # Only if ACFS_DEBUG=1 ``` ## Requirements - All output to stderr (keep stdout clean) - Support --quiet flag (suppress non-errors) - Support --verbose flag (show debug) - Timestamps in log file - ANSI color codes with fallback ## Log File Write all output to: - `/var/log/acfs/install.log` - `~/.acfs/logs/install..log` ## Implementation ```bash #!/bin/bash # scripts/lib/logging.sh ACFS_LOG_FILE="/var/log/acfs/install.log" ACFS_QUIET="${ACFS_QUIET:-0}" ACFS_VERBOSE="${ACFS_VERBOSE:-0}" log_step() { local step="$1" local msg="$2" echo -e "\033[34m[$step] $msg\033[0m" >&2 echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] STEP: $msg" >> "$ACFS_LOG_FILE" } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All log functions implemented - [ ] Colors work in terminal - [ ] --quiet suppresses output - [ ] --verbose shows debug - [ ] Log file written with timestamps - [ ] Works when sourced by other scripts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.871116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.872789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.10","title":"Create modern CLI tools installer (scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Modern CLI Tools Installer ## Description Install modern CLI replacements that the zshrc depends on. ## Tools to Install ### Via apt (Ubuntu 24.04+) - ripgrep (rg) - fast grep - fd-find (fd) - fast find - bat/batcat - better cat - fzf - fuzzy finder - tmux - terminal multiplexer - neovim - better vim ### Via cargo (more recent versions) - lsd - modern ls with icons - dust - better du - btop - better top (or via apt) - zoxide - better cd - ast-grep (sg) - structural grep ### Via curl/installer - lazygit - git TUI - lazydocker - docker TUI - direnv - directory-specific env ## Functions ```bash install_apt_cli_tools() install_cargo_cli_tools() install_other_cli_tools() verify_cli_tools() ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All tools installed - [ ] Fallback logic (apt first, cargo if newer needed) - [ ] Verification for each tool - [ ] Idempotent","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.469899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:27:23.299944-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:27:23.299944-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh with modular installer functions for apt, cargo, and curl-based CLI tools","labels":["installer","mvp","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.10","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.472277-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
